channel.title: Github on MoiSoto's Dev Corner
channel.description: Recent content in Github on MoiSoto's Dev Corner

item.title: Microsoft Build 2026 Resources
item.description:
Microsoft Build was very interesting this year, and focused heavily on AI. Here are my favorite resources from the conference.

Click here if you want to Star or Follow the Gist:

Microsoft Build 2026 Resources · GitHub.

or check below for the embedded version…

item.title: WWDC26 Resources
item.description:
WWDC26 is over! This year is all about Apple Intelligence.
Here is a Gist with my favorite resources from the conference.

Click here if you want to Star or Follow the Gist:

WWDC 2026 Resources · GitHub.

or check below for the embedded version…

item.title: Generative AI Resources
item.description:
Recently I’ve been working on some projects related to Generative AI models. The more I dive into this, the more I realize the vast amount of resources available.

This article will host a Gist that will be updated (hopefully frequently) with the most interesting resources I come across.

Click here if you want to Star or Follow the Gist:

Generative AI Resources · GitHub.

or check below for the embedded version…

item.title: Working with Private Repositories
item.description:
If you are a Github user wanting to host privates repositories, you may wonder what’s the easier way to get them up and running. You may also have to work with different accounts, and soon become aware of how cumbersome this can become, specially if you normally use https for your repos.

This article will show how to setup and use ssh to host private repositories on serveral accounts in a simple and practical way.

item.title: Git Simplified
item.description: We are back after a quite long hiatus, and this time will be posting about Git. After all this is a github web page, so what better topic to resume (and hopefully continue) regular posts proadcast.

item.title: This Hugo Website Is Public!
item.description:
The GitHub repository used for to generate this website is public. And the README.md file contains lots of info on how it was created.

Please check it out at:

https://github.com/moisoto/github-page.dev

item.title: Swift Programming Notes
item.description:
I decided to switch from Objective-C to Swift not long ago, and recently updated some notes for Swift 5.4 to include some new language features and share them on a gist on my main GitHub account.

You can check it here:

Swift Playground Examples for Beginners · GitHub.
